clues.clue: In 'Toy Story,' the cowboy Woody's chief rival turned best friend is this space ranger.
In 'Finding Nemo,' this forgetful blue tang helps Marlin search for his son.
In 'Monsters, Inc.,' this short, one-eyed green monster is Sulley's wisecracking partner.
In 'Up,' this golden retriever wears a collar that lets him speak aloud.
In 'Ratatouille,' the rat Remy secretly steers this clumsy kitchen garbage boy by his hair.
This calm fourth-grader with a football-shaped head lived in a big city boarding house.
In a 1991 Klasky-Csupo series, this screwdriver-wielding baby in a blue shirt led the diaper crew.
This journal-keeping cartoon kid crushed on Patti Mayonnaise and had an alter ego, Quailman.
Two dam-building brothers named Norbert and Daggett were this buck-toothed rodent.
This 1998 Nicktoon starred conjoined feline-and-canine title characters sharing one body.
This live-music city is the capital of Texas.
This city on the American River is California's capital.
This upstate city, not the largest one, is the capital of New York.
This Panhandle city is the capital of Florida.
This city, sharing a witch-trial name, is the capital of Oregon.
This green-haired clown prince of crime is Batman's archenemy.
This Spider-Man foe is a scientist fused with four mechanical tentacle arms.
This bald billionaire businessman is Superman's brilliant nemesis.
This trickster god, Thor's adopted brother, led the Chitauri attack on New York.
The mad titan Thanos sought to gather all six of these reality-warping gems.
Chess is won by trapping the enemy king in this inescapable position.
In Monopoly, you win once every rival has gone this, out of money.
In Scrabble, playing all seven of your tiles at once earns this 50-point bonus.
Rolling five dice that all show the same number scores this game's namesake 50 points.
In Risk, victory means your armies occupy every one of these regions on the map.

final.clue: This chain once promised '30 minutes or it's free' and uses a red-and-blue domino tile logo.
